brief: Explain how our payments retry pipeline works and what changes in the Q3 migration — platform engineering audience.

arguments.contentBrief: Explain how our payments retry pipeline works and what changes in the Q3 migration — platform engineering audience.

structuredContent.rationale: Selected 'cutover' (deck-cloud-migration, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [technical]; matched intent keyword(s) [plan, cloud, migration]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The file-header cover — draw.io file/rev, programme, editors, the two-line thesis, and the notice.

spec.name: deck.code
spec.guidance: The short programme code shown first on every kicker row, e.g. "CBM-CUTOVER".

slots.guidance: The short programme code shown first on every kicker row, e.g. "CBM-CUTOVER".

spec.name: deck.world
spec.guidance: The world name paired with the code on the kicker and the browser title, e.g. "The Cutover".

slots.guidance: The world name paired with the code on the kicker and the browser title, e.g. "The Cutover".

spec.name: deck.file
spec.guidance: The draw.io working-file name, e.g. "cutover-plan.drawio" — shown in the file tab and every kicker row.

slots.guidance: The draw.io working-file name, e.g. "cutover-plan.drawio" — shown in the file tab and every kicker row.

spec.name: deck.rev
spec.guidance: The revision marker, e.g. "rev 14" — paired with the file name and echoed on sign-off.

slots.guidance: The revision marker, e.g. "rev 14" — paired with the file name and echoed on sign-off.

spec.name: deck.programme
spec.guidance: Programme line above the thesis, e.g. "CORE BANKING · CLOUD MIGRATION".

slots.guidance: Programme line above the thesis, e.g. "CORE BANKING · CLOUD MIGRATION".

spec.name: deck.editors
spec.guidance: The draw.io editors line, e.g. "A. VOSS · L. CHEN · PLATFORM".

slots.guidance: The draw.io editors line, e.g. "A. VOSS · L. CHEN · PLATFORM".

spec.name: deck.notice
spec.guidance: REQUIRED synthetic-estate notice printed on every slide foot and the footer.

slots.guidance: REQUIRED synthetic-estate notice printed on every slide foot and the footer.

spec.name: thesis.line1
spec.guidance: First display line of the cover thesis, e.g. "Move everything.".

slots.guidance: First display line of the cover thesis, e.g. "Move everything.".

spec.name: thesis.line2
spec.guidance: Second display line — the fixed-point twist, e.g. "Except one box.".

slots.guidance: Second display line — the fixed-point twist, e.g. "Except one box.".

spec.name: thesis.standfirst
spec.guidance: One paragraph framing the migration around its single fixed point.

slots.guidance: One paragraph framing the migration around its single fixed point.

sections.purpose: The current estate diagram on the shared draw.io canvas; selection handles on the focus node.

spec.name: nodes
spec.guidance: The estate systems (id, label, kind app|data|integration, target zone, disposition). Geometry (current cx/cy + target tx/ty) is OPTIONAL and bounded to the canvas when given; omit it and the template auto-lays the node by zone (on-prem left, cloud right). Exactly one carries disposition "stays" — the padlocked anomaly, in the same place on both estates.

slots.guidance: The estate systems (id, label, kind app|data|integration, target zone, disposition). Geometry (current cx/cy + target tx/ty) is OPTIONAL and bounded to the canvas when given; omit it and the template auto-lays the node by zone (on-prem left, cloud right). Exactly one carries disposition "stays" — the padlocked anomaly, in the same place on both estates.

spec.name: currentEdges
spec.guidance: Current-estate orthogonal connectors: from/to node ids, OPTIONAL exit/enter ports (l|r|t|b) derived from node positions when omitted, and an optional wire label (e.g. "sql · 4ms").

slots.guidance: Current-estate orthogonal connectors: from/to node ids, OPTIONAL exit/enter ports (l|r|t|b) derived from node positions when omitted, and an optional wire label (e.g. "sql · 4ms").

spec.name: currentFocus
spec.guidance: Node id given draw.io selection handles on the current estate slide — usually the fixed point.

slots.guidance: Node id given draw.io selection handles on the current estate slide — usually the fixed point.

spec.name: estateNotes.current
spec.guidance: One-line caption under the current-estate diagram — what the estate is today and which node is the selected fixed point.

slots.guidance: One-line caption under the current-estate diagram — what the estate is today and which node is the selected fixed point.

sections.purpose: The target estate diagram — same canvas, moved; the on-prem zone boxes the node that stays.

spec.name: targetEdges
spec.guidance: Target-estate orthogonal connectors after the move; same shape as currentEdges (ports optional, derived when omitted).

slots.guidance: Target-estate orthogonal connectors after the move; same shape as currentEdges (ports optional, derived when omitted).

spec.name: targetFocus
spec.guidance: Node id given selection handles on the target estate slide — usually the system being refactored.

slots.guidance: Node id given selection handles on the target estate slide — usually the system being refactored.

spec.name: estateNotes.target
spec.guidance: One-line caption under the target-estate diagram — what changes after the move; the fixed point stays boxed in its on-prem zone.

slots.guidance: One-line caption under the target-estate diagram — what changes after the move; the fixed point stays boxed in its on-prem zone.

sections.purpose: Three columns — what moves, what dies, what stays.

spec.name: delta.moves
spec.guidance: Systems that migrate; each a system name and a one-line disposition note.

slots.guidance: Systems that migrate; each a system name and a one-line disposition note.

spec.name: delta.dies
spec.guidance: Systems retired or replaced; each a system name and a one-line note.

slots.guidance: Systems retired or replaced; each a system name and a one-line note.

spec.name: delta.stays
spec.guidance: Systems that stay put; the fixed point belongs here.

slots.guidance: Systems that stay put; the fixed point belongs here.

sections.purpose: The migration waves as a swimlane — each a weekend, its systems as chips.

spec.name: waves
spec.guidance: Each wave has a name, a when, one-to-four system chips (label + kind), and a one-line note.

slots.guidance: Each wave has a name, a when, one-to-four system chips (label + kind), and a one-line note.

sections.purpose: The cutover-night sequence as a comp.flow-diagram, one path down to a validation gate.

spec.name: cutoverFlow.nodes
spec.guidance: Flow steps (id, label, kind start|process|decision|end|data); include one decision gate that branches to open vs. rollback.

slots.guidance: Flow steps (id, label, kind start|process|decision|end|data); include one decision gate that branches to open vs. rollback.

spec.name: cutoverFlow.edges
spec.guidance: Flow transitions (from/to node ids); label the decision branches (e.g. "pass" / "fail").

slots.guidance: Flow transitions (from/to node ids); label the decision branches (e.g. "pass" / "fail").

sections.purpose: The data sync & validation plan as a numbered list.

spec.name: syncPlan
spec.guidance: Ordered sync stages; each a stage name and a one-line detail — nothing cuts over until the data agrees.

slots.guidance: Ordered sync stages; each a stage name and a one-line detail — nothing cuts over until the data agrees.

sections.purpose: The bespoke rollback tree from the validation gate.

spec.name: rollback.nodes
spec.guidance: Rollback-tree nodes (id, label, tone root|ok|abort; optional x/y auto-laid by depth when omitted); the root is the validation gate.

slots.guidance: Rollback-tree nodes (id, label, tone root|ok|abort; optional x/y auto-laid by depth when omitted); the root is the validation gate.

spec.name: rollback.edges
spec.guidance: Rollback-tree edges (from/to node ids) — the pass branch and the fail chain.

slots.guidance: Rollback-tree edges (from/to node ids) — the pass branch and the fail chain.

spec.name: rollback.note
spec.guidance: One paragraph on why rollback is bounded to the maintenance window.

slots.guidance: One paragraph on why rollback is bounded to the maintenance window.

sections.purpose: The cutover risk register as a comp.status-list.

spec.name: risks
spec.guidance: Each risk: a label, a status (success|warning|danger|info|neutral), and a description.

slots.guidance: Each risk: a label, a status (success|warning|danger|info|neutral), and a description.

sections.purpose: The rev sign-off — the decisions this review must grant.

spec.name: signoff.title
spec.guidance: The sign-off headline, e.g. "Ready to sign rev 14.".

slots.guidance: The sign-off headline, e.g. "Ready to sign rev 14.".

spec.name: signoff.detail
spec.guidance: One paragraph stating what the review must decide.

slots.guidance: One paragraph stating what the review must decide.

spec.name: signoff.approvals
spec.guidance: Each approval: a role and the decision it must make, with a sign-off box.

slots.guidance: Each approval: a role and the decision it must make, with a sign-off box.

craftGuarantees: The draw.io working-file idiom: a flat dot-grid canvas, exact orthogonal connectors with port dots, pastel-filled system boxes with type badges, a layers legend chip in the chrome, and selection handles on each slide’s focus node.

Exactly one estate node carries the "stays" anomaly disposition: padlocked, heavier-stroked, in the SAME place on both the current and target estate; its badge is the verbatim anomaly text, echoed on both estate slides.
The synthetic-estate notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.

Both estates expose a hidden zone-grouped accessible mirror derived from the same node data — the current estate reads all on-prem, the target splits into a cloud landing zone plus the on-prem zone that keeps the fixed point.
Node and edge counts are bounded so the shared estate canvas, the swimlanes, and the flow diagram stay composed for any schema-valid fill.
Motion level 2 (data-ink draw); the mood is locked light.

craftGuarantees: The draw.io working-file idiom: a flat dot-grid canvas, exact orthogonal connectors with port dots, pastel-filled system boxes with type badges, a layers legend chip in the chrome, and selection handles on each slide’s focus node.

Exactly one estate node carries the "stays" anomaly disposition: padlocked, heavier-stroked, in the SAME place on both the current and target estate; its badge is the verbatim anomaly text, echoed on both estate slides.
The synthetic-estate notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.

Both estates expose a hidden zone-grouped accessible mirror derived from the same node data — the current estate reads all on-prem, the target splits into a cloud landing zone plus the on-prem zone that keeps the fixed point.
Node and edge counts are bounded so the shared estate canvas, the swimlanes, and the flow diagram stay composed for any schema-valid fill.
Motion level 2 (data-ink draw); the mood is locked light.

text.rationale: Selected 'cutover' (deck-cloud-migration, art-directed style); shares audience(s) [technical]; matched intent keyword(s) [plan, cloud, migration]; art-directed style fits standard corporate suitability; motion preference 2 noted (the template locks its own motion).

sections.purpose: The file-header cover — draw.io file/rev, programme, editors, the two-line thesis, and the notice.

sections.purpose: The current estate diagram on the shared draw.io canvas; selection handles on the focus node.

spec.name: nodes
spec.guidance: The estate systems (id, label, kind app|data|integration, target zone, disposition). Geometry (current cx/cy + target tx/ty) is OPTIONAL and bounded to the canvas when given; omit it and the template auto-lays the node by zone (on-prem left, cloud right). Exactly one carries disposition "stays" — the padlocked anomaly, in the same place on both estates.

slots.guidance: The estate systems (id, label, kind app|data|integration, target zone, disposition). Geometry (current cx/cy + target tx/ty) is OPTIONAL and bounded to the canvas when given; omit it and the template auto-lays the node by zone (on-prem left, cloud right). Exactly one carries disposition "stays" — the padlocked anomaly, in the same place on both estates.

Exactly one estate node carries the "stays" anomaly disposition: padlocked, heavier-stroked, in the SAME place on both the current and target estate; its badge is the verbatim anomaly text, echoed on both estate slides.
The synthetic-estate notice is required and prints on every slide’s print foot and the footer.

deck.editors: R. OKORO · M. IQBAL · PAYMENTS PLATFORM

thesis.standfirst: Six systems in the payments retry pipeline leave the data centre over three weekends. One does not: the settlement ledger stays on-prem — the legal book of record, with a 5ms posting SLA. The whole plan is shaped around that single fixed point.

estateNotes.current: Six systems in one data centre, hung off a retry orchestrator that posts every settlement to the ledger. Selected: the ledger — the fixed point.
estateNotes.target: Same canvas, moved. The orchestrator refactors into the cloud; the nightly batch poster retires; the ledger stays exactly where it is, on-prem.

nodes.label: Settlement ledger
nodes.badge: SETTLEMENT LEDGER — STAYS ON-PREM · BOOK OF RECORD 5ms SLA

moves.note: Rehost to managed containers, no code change.
Refactor into a stateless service as it migrates.
Replatform onto the managed streaming bus.

dies.note: Retired — replaced by streaming replay from the DLQ.
Replaced by the managed scheduler; cron becomes queues.

stays.note: Stays on-prem. The legal book of record; 5ms posting SLA.

waves.name: Wave 1 · Edge
waves.note: Stateless intake, low blast-radius — proves the pipeline and the runbook end to end.

waves.name: Wave 2 · Core
waves.note: The hard one. The orchestrator moves while it keeps its 5ms posting link back to the ledger.

waves.name: Wave 3 · Data
waves.note: Reconciliation replatformed; the nightly batch retires once streaming replay is verified.

syncPlan.detail: DLQ backlog and retry-state store snapshotted to the target ahead of the window.
CDC streams retry-state deltas until source authorizations are paused.
Settled totals matched against the on-prem ledger, source vs target.
Target posts to the ledger in shadow; every result diffed for a full week.
Zero unexplained settlement diffs for 72h before live traffic cuts over.

rollback.note: Rollback is bounded to the maintenance window: the on-prem retry path is never more than a frozen delta behind, so a failed gate returns payments to the old pipeline inside the same night.

risks.label: Cross-link latency to the ledger
risks.description: The orchestrator in cloud must still post to the on-prem ledger within 5ms. A dedicated low-latency link is provisioned and measured under peak retry load, not assumed.

risks.label: Duplicate settlement on retry
risks.description: Idempotency keys are carried end to end; DLQ replay is exactly-once against the ledger, verified by the week-long shadow-post diff before any cutover.

risks.label: Window overrun
risks.description: Each wave is rehearsed end to end in staging; the runbook is timed with 40% headroom.

risks.label: Rollback confidence
risks.description: Rollback executed cleanly in two rehearsals; the gateway repoint and pause steps are automated and timed.

signoff.title: Ready to sign rev 9.
signoff.detail: The pipeline moves around one fixed point: the settlement ledger stays on-prem. Three waves, one rehearsed cutover runbook, one bounded rollback. What we need from this review:

approvals.decision: Confirm the ledger stays on-prem at the 5ms posting SLA.

checks.name: compose_slide_deck structuredContent is a valid ComposeSlideDeckOutput
compose_slide_deck fill skeleton carries slide kinds + craft guarantees
validate_fill structuredContent is a valid ValidateFillOutput
validate_fill(sample payments-retry fill) valid=true, no findings
